clusters.title: Log tail output is fully buffered in memory

member_findings.title: Log tail output is fully buffered in memory
member_findings.impact: Because -n accepts any positive integer, an operator can request a very large tail from a large worker log; capture_output=True stores the full stdout in the dscli process before printing, so the command can spike memory or be killed instead of streaming the log.
member_findings.fix: Stream tail stdout directly to the console, for example by leaving stdout inherited and only capturing stderr, or add a documented upper bound for -n before invoking tail.
